note: Auto-generated with full schemas from tool decorators. Do not edit manually.

tools.description: Adopt a pending device into the Unifi Network by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_adopt_device

confirm.description: When true, initiates device adoption. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the pending device to adopt, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices with status='pending')

tools.description: Archive (resolve/dismiss) a specific alarm by its ID
tools.name: unifi_archive_alarm

alarm_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the alarm to archive (from unifi_list_alarms)

confirm.description: When true, archives the alarm. When false (default), requires confirmation

tools.description: Archive (resolve/dismiss) all active alarms
tools.name: unifi_archive_all_alarms

confirm.description: When true, archives all active alarms. When false (default), requires confirmation

tools.description: Authorize a guest client to access the guest network by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_authorize_guest

bytes_quota.description: Total data transfer quota in bytes. Omit for unlimited

confirm.description: When true, executes the authorization. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

down_kbps.description: Download bandwidth limit in Kbps (e.g., 10000 for 10 Mbps). Omit for unlimited

mac_address.description: MAC address of the guest client to authorize, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF

minutes.description: Duration in minutes the guest is authorized (default 1440 = 24 hours)

up_kbps.description: Upload bandwidth limit in Kbps (e.g., 5000 for 5 Mbps). Omit for unlimited

tools.description: Block a client/device from the network by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_block_client

confirm.description: When true, executes the block. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to block, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

tools.description: Configure link aggregation (LACP/LAG) on a switch. Bonds consecutive ports for increased bandwidth. Most switches support up to 8 aggregate sessions. Ports must be sequential and same speed. The master port override must include: op_mode='aggregate', aggregate_members=[list of port indices], and lag_idx=<group number starting at 1>. Member ports (all except the master) must be REMOVED from the overrides array — the controller auto-manages them. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_configure_port_aggregation

confirm.description: When true, applies the LAG config. When false (default), returns a preview

port_overrides.description: Complete port overrides array. The master port must have op_mode='aggregate', aggregate_members=[port_idx, port_idx+1, ...], and lag_idx=<int>. Remove member ports from the array — the controller claims them automatically

tools.description: Configure port mirroring on a switch. Mirrors traffic from one port to a destination port for packet capture/analysis. Most switches support only 1 mirror session. Fetch current overrides with unifi_get_switch_ports, set op_mode='mirror' and mirror_port_idx on the source port, then send all overrides. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_configure_port_mirror

confirm.description: When true, applies the mirror config. When false (default), returns a preview

port_overrides.description: Complete port overrides array. The source port must have op_mode='mirror' and mirror_port_idx='<destination_port_idx>'

tools.description: Create a new MAC ACL rule for Layer 2 access control within a VLAN. Uses the same field names as unifi_list_acl_rules output — source_macs, destination_macs, network_id, action, etc. Empty MAC list = match any device. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_acl_rule

acl_index.description: Position in the rule chain (lower numbers are evaluated first)

confirm.description: When true, creates the rule. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

destination_macs.description: List of destination MAC addresses to match (empty list = any destination)

enabled.description: Whether the rule is active (default: true)

name.description: Descriptive name for the ACL rule

network_id.description: Network/VLAN ID this rule applies to (from unifi_list_networks)

source_macs.description: List of source MAC addresses to match (empty list = any source)

tools.description: Create a new AP group to control which APs broadcast which SSIDs. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_ap_group

confirm.description: When true, creates the AP group. When false (default), returns a preview

group_data.description: AP group configuration dict. Typical fields: name (str), device_macs (list of AP MAC addresses), wlan_group_ids (list of WLAN group IDs)

tools.description: Create a new backup of the controller configuration. Returns the backup metadata on success. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_backup

confirm.description: When true, creates the backup. When false (default), returns a preview

tools.description: Create a new client group (network member group). Groups organize devices by MAC address for use in OON policies and firewall rules. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_client_group

confirm.description: When true, creates the group. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

members.description: List of MAC addresses to include in the group (e.g., ['aa:bb:cc:dd:ee:ff', '11:22:33:44:55:66'])

name.description: Descriptive name for the group (e.g., 'Kids All', 'Work Laptops')

tools.description: Create a new static DNS record. Required: key (hostname, e.g. 'myhost.example.com'), value (IP or target hostname), record_type ('A'/'AAAA'/'CNAME'/'MX'/'TXT'/'SRV'). Optional: enabled (bool, default true), ttl (int, 0=default 300s), port (int, for SRV), priority (int, for MX/SRV), weight (int, for SRV). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_dns_record

confirm.description: When true, creates the record. When false (default), returns a preview

record_data.description: DNS record data. See tool description for required and optional fields.

tools.description: Create a new firewall group (address or port group). group_type must be 'address-group' (for IPs/CIDRs), 'ipv6-address-group', or 'port-group' (for port numbers/ranges). IMPORTANT: group_type cannot be changed after creation. group_members format: addresses use ['10.0.0.1', '10.0.0.0/24'], ports use ['80', '443', '8080-8090']. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_firewall_group

confirm.description: When true, creates the group. When false (default), returns a preview

group_members.description: List of IPs/CIDRs (for address groups) or port numbers/ranges (for port groups)

tools.description: Create a new firewall policy with schema validation. Supports both legacy ruleset-based policies (action: accept/drop/reject) and zone-based policies (action: ALLOW/BLOCK/REJECT with source/destination zone_id targeting). Format is auto-detected from the policy_data structure.
tools.name: unifi_create_firewall_policy

confirm.description: When true, creates the policy. When false (default), validates and returns a preview

policy_data.description:
Firewall policy configuration dict. Two formats supported:
Legacy (ruleset-based): Required: name, ruleset (WAN_IN, LAN_OUT, etc.), action (accept/drop/reject), index. Optional: enabled, description, protocol, connection_states, source, destination, logging.
Zone-based (firmware 5.0+): Required: name, action (ALLOW/BLOCK/REJECT), source (object with zone_id, matching_target), destination (same structure). For IP targeting: matching_target='IP', matching_target_type='SPECIFIC', ips=[...]. For network targeting: matching_target='NETWORK', matching_target_type='OBJECT', network_ids=[...]. For any in zone: matching_target='ANY'.

tools.description: Create a new network (LAN/VLAN) with schema validation. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_network

confirm.description: When true, creates the network. When false (default), validates and returns a preview

network_data.description: Network configuration dict. Required: name (str), purpose (str: 'corporate', 'guest', 'wan', 'vlan-only', 'vpn-client', 'vpn-server'). Required if purpose != 'vlan-only': ip_subnet (CIDR, e.g. '192.168.1.0/24'). Required if purpose == 'vlan-only': vlan (int 1-4094). Optional: vlan_enabled, vlan, dhcpd_enabled, dhcpd_start, dhcpd_stop, dhcpd_leasetime, domain_name, enabled, network_isolation_enabled, upnp_lan_enabled. See update_network for the full list of supported DHCP/DNS fields.

tools.description: Create a new OON (Object-Oriented Network) policy for internet access scheduling, app blocking, bandwidth limiting, or VPN routing. IMPORTANT: qos and route must be full nested dicts (not the flattened qos_enabled/route_enabled booleans from list output). Use unifi_get_oon_policy_details on an existing policy as a template for the nested structure. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_oon_policy

confirm.description: When true, creates the policy. When false (default), returns a preview

qos.description: Bandwidth limiting config. Must include mode ('OFF' or 'LIMIT') and related fields even if disabled

route.description: VPN routing config. Must include mode ('OFF' or a VPN interface) and related fields even if disabled

secure.description: Internet access and app blocking config. Keys: internet_access_enabled (bool), apps (list of blocked app objects), schedule (access schedule)

target_type.description: Target type: 'CLIENTS' (target by MAC) or 'GROUPS' (target by group ID)

targets.description: List of target MAC addresses (if CLIENTS) or group IDs (if GROUPS)

tools.description: Create a new port forwarding rule on your Unifi Network controller using schema validation.
tools.name: unifi_create_port_forward

port_forward_data.description: Port forward configuration dict. Required: name (str), dst_port (external port, e.g. '80' or '10000-10010'), fwd_port (internal port), fwd_ip (internal IP, e.g. '192.168.1.100'). Optional: protocol ('tcp'/'udp'/'tcp_udp', default 'tcp_udp'), enabled (bool, default true), src_ip (source IP/CIDR), log (bool)

tools.description: Create a new port profile. forward values: 'native' (access port), 'all' (trunk), 'customize' (selective trunk), 'disabled'. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_port_profile

confirm.description: When true, creates the profile. When false (default), returns a preview

tools.description: Create a new QoS rule on the Unifi Network controller. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_qos_rule

confirm.description: When true, creates the rule. When false (default), validates and returns a preview

qos_data.description: QoS rule configuration dict. Required: name (str), interface ('WAN'/'LAN'), direction ('upload'/'download'), bandwidth_limit_kbps (int). Optional: target_ip_address (IP), target_subnet (CIDR), dscp_value (0-63), enabled (bool, default true)

tools.description:
Create a new static route for advanced routing configuration.

Specify destination network in CIDR format (e.g., "10.0.0.0/24") and
next-hop IP address (e.g., "192.168.1.1").
tools.name: unifi_create_route

confirm.description: When true, creates the route. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

distance.description: Administrative distance / route metric (1-255, default 1, lower = preferred)

enabled.description: Whether the route is active (default true)

name.description: Descriptive name for the static route (e.g., 'Office subnet via VPN')

tools.description: Create a firewall policy using a simplified high-level schema. Accepts friendly src/dst selectors and returns a preview unless confirm=true.
tools.name: unifi_create_simple_firewall_policy

confirm.description: When true, creates the policy. When false (default), validates and returns a preview

policy.description: Simplified firewall policy dict. Required: name (str), ruleset (str: 'LAN_OUT', 'WAN_IN', etc.), action (str: 'drop', 'accept', 'reject'), src (dict with type+value), dst (dict with type+value). src/dst type options: 'zone' (value: 'wan'/'lan'), 'network' (value: network name/ID), 'client_mac' (value: MAC address), 'ip_group' (value: group ID). Optional: protocol, index, enabled, log

tools.description: Create a port forward using a simplified schema. Returns a preview unless confirm=true.
tools.name: unifi_create_simple_port_forward

confirm.description: When true, creates the rule. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

rule.description: Simplified port forward dict. Required: name (str), ext_port (external port, e.g. '8443'), to_ip (internal IP, e.g. '192.168.1.10'). Optional: int_port (internal port, defaults to ext_port), protocol ('tcp'/'udp'/'both', default 'both'), enabled (bool, default true)

tools.description: Create a QoS rule using a simplified high-level schema. Returns a preview unless confirm=true.
tools.name: unifi_create_simple_qos_rule

confirm.description: When true, creates the rule. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

rule.description: Simplified QoS rule dict. Required: name (str), interface ('wan'/'lan'), direction ('upload'/'download'), limit_kbps (int). Optional: enabled (bool, default true), dscp_value (int 0-63), target (dict with type='ip'/'subnet' and value, e.g. {'type': 'ip', 'value': '192.168.1.50'})

tools.description:
Create a new user group (bandwidth profile) with optional speed limits.

Bandwidth limits are specified in Kbps. Use -1 for unlimited.
User groups can be assigned to clients to enforce bandwidth policies.
tools.name: unifi_create_usergroup

confirm.description: When true, creates the user group. When false (default), returns a preview

down_limit_kbps.description: Download speed limit in Kbps (e.g., 10000 for 10 Mbps). Use -1 for unlimited. Omit to leave uncapped

name.description: Name for the user group / bandwidth profile

up_limit_kbps.description: Upload speed limit in Kbps (e.g., 5000 for 5 Mbps). Use -1 for unlimited. Omit to leave uncapped

tools.description:
Create hotspot voucher(s) for guest network access.

Vouchers can have:
- Time limits: How long the voucher is valid after activation
- Usage quota: Single-use (1), multi-use (0), or n-times usable
- Bandwidth limits: Upload/download speed caps in Kbps
- Data caps: Total data transfer limit in MB
tools.name: unifi_create_voucher

bytes_limit_mb.description: Total data transfer limit in MB. Omit for unlimited

confirm.description: When true, creates the voucher(s). When false (default), returns a preview

count.description: Number of vouchers to create in this batch (1-10000, default 1)

down_limit_kbps.description: Download speed limit in Kbps (e.g., 10000 for 10 Mbps). Omit for unlimited

expire_minutes.description: Duration in minutes the voucher is valid after activation (default 1440 = 24 hours)

note.description: Optional note/label for the voucher batch (e.g., 'Conference guests')

quota.description: Usage limit per voucher: 1 = single-use (default), 0 = unlimited uses, N = N-times usable

up_limit_kbps.description: Upload speed limit in Kbps (e.g., 5000 for 5 Mbps). Omit for unlimited

tools.description: Create a new WireGuard peer on a WireGuard server. The UniFi controller generates the keypair and assigns an IP automatically. Requires confirmation. After creation the peer config (public key, assigned IP) is returned; private keys are redacted -- download the full client config from the UniFi UI.
tools.name: unifi_create_wireguard_peer

confirm.description: When true, creates the peer. When false (default), returns a preview

server_id.description: WireGuard server _id to add the peer to (from unifi_list_vpn_servers)

tools.description: Create a new Wireless LAN (WLAN/SSID) with schema validation. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_create_wlan

confirm.description: When true, creates the WLAN. When false (default), validates and returns a preview

wlan_data.description: WLAN configuration dict. Required: name (SSID string), security ('open'/'wpa-psk'/'wpa2-psk'). Required if security != 'open': x_passphrase (password). Optional: enabled (bool, default true), hide_ssid (bool), guest_policy (bool), usergroup_id, networkconf_id (network to associate)

tools.description: Delete a MAC ACL rule. Requires confirmation. WARNING: Removing an ALLOW rule may block device communication. Removing a BLOCK rule may open access.
tools.name: unifi_delete_acl_rule

confirm.description: When true, deletes the rule. When false (default), returns a preview. WARNING: Removing an ALLOW rule may block device communication

rule_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the ACL rule to delete (from unifi_list_acl_rules)

tools.description: Delete an AP group by ID. Requires confirmation. WARNING: APs in this group may lose their SSID assignments.
tools.name: unifi_delete_ap_group

confirm.description: When true, deletes the AP group. When false (default), returns a preview. WARNING: APs in this group may lose their SSID assignments

group_id.description: Unique identifier of the AP group to delete (from unifi_list_ap_groups)

tools.description: Delete a backup file from the controller. Use unifi_list_backups to find filenames. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_delete_backup

confirm.description: When true, deletes the backup. When false (default), returns a preview

tools.description: Delete a client group. Requires confirmation. WARNING: Deleting a group may affect OON policies and firewall rules that reference it.
tools.name: unifi_delete_client_group

confirm.description: When true, deletes the group. When false (default), returns a preview

group_id.description: The ID of the group to delete

tools.description: Delete a content filtering profile. Requires confirmation. WARNING: Deleting a profile removes DNS-based blocking for all targeted clients/networks.
tools.name: unifi_delete_content_filter

confirm.description: When true, deletes the profile. When false (default), returns a preview

filter_id.description: The ID of the profile to delete

tools.description: Delete a static DNS record. Use unifi_list_dns_records to find record IDs. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_delete_dns_record

confirm.description: When true, deletes the record. When false (default), returns a preview

record_id.description: The ID of the DNS record to delete (from unifi_list_dns_records)

tools.description: Delete a firewall group. Requires confirmation. WARNING: Firewall policies referencing this group via ip_group_id or port_group_id may break.
tools.name: unifi_delete_firewall_group

confirm.description: When true, deletes the group. When false (default), returns a preview

group_id.description: The ID of the group to delete

tools.description: Delete a firewall policy by ID. Requires confirmation. WARNING: Removing an ALLOW rule may block traffic. Removing a BLOCK rule may open access.
tools.name: unifi_delete_firewall_policy

confirm.description: When true, deletes the policy. When false (default), returns a preview. WARNING: Removing an ALLOW rule may block traffic

policy_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the firewall policy to delete (from unifi_list_firewall_policies)

tools.description: Delete an OON policy. Requires confirmation. WARNING: This will remove the policy and all associated firewall rules that were auto-generated from it.
tools.name: unifi_delete_oon_policy

confirm.description: When true, deletes the policy. When false (default), returns a preview

policy_id.description: The ID of the policy to delete

tools.description: Delete a port profile. System profiles with attr_no_delete=true cannot be deleted. WARNING: Switch ports using this profile will revert to defaults. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_delete_port_profile

confirm.description: When true, deletes the profile. When false (default), returns a preview

profile_id.description: The ID of the profile to delete

tools.description: Delete a WireGuard peer by ID. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_delete_wireguard_peer

confirm.description: When true, deletes the peer. When false (default), returns a preview

peer_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WireGuard peer to delete (from unifi_list_wireguard_peers)

tools.description: Delete a WLAN/SSID by ID. Requires confirmation. WARNING: All devices using this SSID will be disconnected.
tools.name: unifi_delete_wlan

confirm.description: When true, deletes the WLAN. When false (default), returns a preview. WARNING: All devices using this SSID will be disconnected

wlan_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WLAN/SSID to delete (from unifi_list_wlans)

tools.description: Force re-provision a device, pushing the current configuration from the controller to the device. Works on any UniFi device (switches, APs, gateways). Useful after manual changes or to resolve config drift. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_force_provision_device

confirm.description: When true, force provisions the device. When false (default), returns a preview

device_mac.description: MAC address of the device (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Force a client to reconnect to the network (kick) by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_force_reconnect_client

confirm.description: When true, executes the forced reconnect. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to force reconnect, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

tools.description: Remove/forget a client from the controller's known client history by MAC address. This permanently deletes the client record including its name, notes, fixed IP settings, and historical stats. The client will reappear as a new unknown device if it reconnects.
tools.name: unifi_forget_client

confirm.description: When true, executes the forget. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to forget, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific MAC ACL rule by ID. Returns the same field names as unifi_list_acl_rules.
tools.name: unifi_get_acl_rule_details

rule_id.description: The id field from unifi_list_acl_rules output

tools.description: Get recent alerts from the Unifi Network controller
tools.name: unifi_get_alerts

include_archived.description: When true, includes previously archived/resolved alerts. Default false

limit.description: Maximum number of alerts to return (default 10)

tools.description: Get network anomaly detection events.
tools.name: unifi_get_anomalies

tools.description: Get details of a specific AP group by ID, including member APs and WLANs.
tools.name: unifi_get_ap_group_details

group_id.description: Unique identifier of the AP group (from unifi_list_ap_groups)

tools.description: Get auto-backup settings (enabled state, schedule, retention count, cloud backup).
tools.name: unifi_get_autobackup_settings

tools.description: Returns the full raw client object for one client by MAC address — includes all controller-reported fields: IP, hostname, connection stats, DHCP info, network/WLAN associations, traffic counters, and fixed-IP settings. For a summary of all clients, use unifi_list_clients.
tools.name: unifi_get_client_details

mac_address.description: Client MAC address in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

tools.description: Get per-client DPI traffic data by application or category. Note: requires Traffic Identification to be enabled on the controller, which is separate from Device Identification.
tools.name: unifi_get_client_dpi_traffic

group_by.description: Group results: 'by_app' (default) for per-application or 'by_cat' for per-category

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific client group by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_client_group_details

group_id.description: The unique identifier of the client group

tools.description: Get client session history. Note: this endpoint tracks hotspot/captive portal authorization sessions, not general WiFi connect/disconnect events. Returns empty without a guest portal configured. For WiFi roaming events, use the event log instead.
tools.name: unifi_get_client_sessions

client_mac.description: Client MAC address. If omitted, returns sessions for all clients.

limit.description: Maximum number of sessions to return (default 50)

tools.description: Get statistics for a specific client/device
tools.name: unifi_get_client_stats

duration.description: Time period for stats: 'hourly' (last 1h, default), 'daily' (24h), 'weekly' (7d), or 'monthly' (30d)

tools.description: Get detailed WiFi statistics for a single wireless client including signal, noise, satisfaction, tx/rx rates, retries, roam count, channel, radio, ESSID, and more.
tools.name: unifi_get_client_wifi_details

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific content filtering profile by ID. Returns the full profile including categories, client MACs, network IDs, safe search settings, and allow/block lists.
tools.name: unifi_get_content_filter_details

filter_id.description: The unique identifier (_id) of the content filtering profile

tools.description: Get the pre-aggregated site dashboard summary (health, device counts, client counts, ISP status).
tools.name: unifi_get_dashboard

tools.description: Returns the full raw device object for one device by MAC address — includes radio tables, port tables, system stats, WAN info, firmware details, and all controller-reported fields. Use when you need deep inspection of a specific device. For a filtered overview of all devices, use unifi_list_devices.
tools.name: unifi_get_device_details

mac_address.description: Device MAC address in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Get radio configuration and live statistics for an access point. Returns per-band config (channel, tx_power, channel width, min_rssi) and live stats (actual tx_power, channel utilization, client count, retries).
tools.name: unifi_get_device_radio

mac_address.description: MAC address of the access point, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices with device_type='ap')

tools.description: Returns historical traffic time-series (rx/tx bytes, client counts) for one device by MAC or _id. Duration: hourly/daily/weekly/monthly. For APs, includes avg/max client counts and WiFi quality metrics. For current device status instead of stats, use unifi_get_device_details.
tools.name: unifi_get_device_stats

duration.description: Time period for stats: 'hourly' (last 1h, default), 'daily' (24h), 'weekly' (7d), or 'monthly' (30d)

tools.description: Get details for a specific DNS record by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_dns_record_details

record_id.description: The unique identifier (_id) of the DNS record

tools.description: Get Deep Packet Inspection (DPI) statistics (applications and categories)
tools.name: unifi_get_dpi_stats

tools.description:
Get a list of known event type prefixes for filtering events.

Use these prefixes with unifi_list_events event_type parameter to filter specific event categories.
tools.name: unifi_get_event_types

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific firewall group by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_firewall_group_details

group_id.description: The unique identifier (_id) of the firewall group

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific firewall policy by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_firewall_policy_details

policy_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the firewall policy (from unifi_list_firewall_policies)

tools.description: Get gateway WAN/LAN performance history including bandwidth, CPU, and memory utilization.
tools.name: unifi_get_gateway_stats

tools.description: Get IPS/IDS security events (intrusion detection/prevention alerts). Note: on newer UniFi OS / Network Application versions, IPS events may not populate this legacy endpoint. Check the controller's traffic flows for threat data instead.
tools.name: unifi_get_ips_events

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 50)

tools.description: Get LLDP neighbor discovery table for a switch. Shows what devices are connected to each port by their LLDP-advertised chassis ID and descriptions. Use unifi_list_devices to find switch MAC addresses.
tools.name: unifi_get_lldp_neighbors

device_mac.description: MAC address of the switch (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Get details for a specific network by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_network_details

network_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the network (from unifi_list_networks)

tools.description: Returns per-subsystem health status for WAN, LAN, WLAN, and VPN — each with status, number of gateways/switches/APs, and active user counts. Use to check WAN connectivity, see how many devices are online per subsystem, or detect degraded network segments. For controller-level info (version, uptime), use unifi_get_system_info.
tools.name: unifi_get_network_health

tools.description: Get network statistics from the Unifi Network controller
tools.name: unifi_get_network_stats

duration.description: Time period for stats: 'hourly' (last 1h, default), 'daily' (24h), 'weekly' (7d), or 'monthly' (30d)

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific OON policy by ID. Returns the full policy object including secure, qos, route sections and targets.
tools.name: unifi_get_oon_policy_details

policy_id.description: The unique identifier of the OON policy

tools.description: Get a specific port forwarding rule by ID from your Unifi Network controller.
tools.name: unifi_get_port_forward

port_forward_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the port forwarding rule (from unifi_list_port_forwards)

tools.description: Get detailed configuration for a specific port profile by ID. Returns full profile including VLAN, isolation, PoE, STP, 802.1X, storm control, and QoS settings.
tools.name: unifi_get_port_profile_details

profile_id.description: The unique identifier (_id) of the port profile

tools.description: Get live port statistics for a switch. Returns per-port data including link speed, duplex, PoE draw (voltage/current/power), error counters, MAC table count, bytes per second, and enabled state. Use unifi_list_devices to find switch MAC addresses.
tools.name: unifi_get_port_stats

device_mac.description: MAC address of the switch (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Get details for a specific QoS rule by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_qos_rule_details

rule_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the QoS rule (from unifi_list_qos_rules)

tools.description: Get RF spectrum scan results for an access point. Returns per-channel interference levels (dBm), utilization (%), and BSS counts for each radio band. Trigger a scan first with unifi_trigger_rf_scan if no results are available. Scans take 5-10 minutes — if spectrum_scanning is true, the scan is still in progress.
tools.name: unifi_get_rf_scan_results

ap_mac.description: MAC address of the access point, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices with device_type='ap')

tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific static route by its ID
tools.name: unifi_get_route_details

route_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the static route (from unifi_list_routes)

tools.description: Get actual DPI traffic data by application or category for the entire site. Shows real bandwidth usage per app/category, NOT restriction configuration. Use unifi_get_dpi_stats for restriction config instead. Note: requires Traffic Identification to be enabled on the controller, which is separate from Device Identification.
tools.name: unifi_get_site_dpi_traffic

group_by.description: Group results: 'by_app' (default) for per-application or 'by_cat' for per-category

tools.description: Get current site settings (e.g., country code, timezone, connectivity monitoring).
tools.name: unifi_get_site_settings

tools.description: Get current SNMP settings for the site (enabled state, community string).
tools.name: unifi_get_snmp_settings

tools.description: Get historical speedtest results including download, upload (Mbps), and latency (ms).
tools.name: unifi_get_speedtest_results

tools.description: Check the status of a running speedtest on the gateway.
tools.name: unifi_get_speedtest_status

gateway_mac.description: MAC address of the gateway device

tools.description: Get switch hardware capabilities including max ACL rules, max VLANs, max aggregation sessions, max mirror sessions, STP config, and jumbo frame status. Use unifi_list_devices to find switch MAC addresses.
tools.name: unifi_get_switch_capabilities

device_mac.description: MAC address of the switch (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Get port assignments for a specific switch. Shows which port profile is assigned to each port, port names, and PoE mode. Use unifi_list_devices to find switch MAC addresses.
tools.name: unifi_get_switch_ports

device_mac.description: MAC address of the switch (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Returns controller version, uptime, hostname, memory/CPU usage, and update availability. Use for basic 'is the controller healthy?' checks. For network subsystem status (WAN/LAN/WLAN), use unifi_get_network_health instead. For network subsystem details, use unifi_get_network_health.
tools.name: unifi_get_system_info

tools.description: Get a list of top clients by usage (sorted by total bytes)
tools.name: unifi_get_top_clients

duration.description: Time period for stats: 'hourly' (1h), 'daily' (24h, default), 'weekly' (7d), or 'monthly' (30d)

limit.description: Maximum number of top clients to return (default 10)

tools.description: Get detailed information for a specific traffic route by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_traffic_route_details

route_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the traffic route (from unifi_list_traffic_routes)

tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific user group by its ID
tools.name: unifi_get_usergroup_details

group_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the user group (from unifi_list_usergroups)

tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific voucher by its ID
tools.name: unifi_get_voucher_details

voucher_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the voucher (from unifi_list_vouchers)

tools.description: Get details for a specific VPN client by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_vpn_client_details

client_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the VPN client (from unifi_list_vpn_clients)

tools.description: Get details for a specific VPN server by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_vpn_server_details

server_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the VPN server (from unifi_list_vpn_servers)

tools.description: Get details for a specific WireGuard peer by ID. Private keys are redacted.
tools.name: unifi_get_wireguard_peer

peer_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WireGuard peer (from unifi_list_wireguard_peers)

tools.description: Get details for a specific WLAN by ID.
tools.name: unifi_get_wlan_details

wlan_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WLAN/SSID (from unifi_list_wlans)

tools.description: List MAC ACL rules (Policy Engine) for Layer 2 access control. These rules control which devices can communicate at Layer 2 within a VLAN.
tools.name: unifi_list_acl_rules

network_id.description: Filter rules by network/VLAN ID (from unifi_list_networks). Omit to list all ACL rules

tools.description:
List all active routes from the device routing table.

Includes both user-defined and system routes currently in effect.
This shows the actual routing table state on the gateway device.

Note: This endpoint may not be available on all controller versions.
Returns empty list if unavailable.
tools.name: unifi_list_active_routes

tools.description: Returns active alarms (security alerts, connectivity issues, firmware warnings). Each alarm includes type, message, timestamp, and related device/client MAC. By default shows only unresolved alarms; set include_archived=true for history. For general event logs (non-critical), use unifi_list_events.
tools.name: unifi_list_alarms

include_archived.description: When true, includes previously resolved/archived alarms. Default false (active only)

limit.description: Maximum number of alarms to return (default 100)

tools.description: List all AP groups configured on the controller. AP groups control which access points broadcast which SSIDs. Returns group names, IDs, and associated AP/WLAN memberships.
tools.name: unifi_list_ap_groups

tools.description: List allowed RF channels for the site's regulatory domain. Returns per-band channel lists with DFS status and max power. Useful for planning channel assignments.
tools.name: unifi_list_available_channels

tools.description: List available backups on the controller. Returns filename, datetime, size, and version for each backup.
tools.name: unifi_list_backups

tools.description: List clients/devices that are currently blocked from the network
tools.name: unifi_list_blocked_clients

tools.description: List client groups (network member groups). Groups organize devices by MAC address for use in OON policies and firewall rules.
tools.name: unifi_list_client_groups

tools.description: Returns connected clients with MAC, name, hostname, IP, connection type (wired/wireless), and for wireless clients: SSID, signal dBm, channel, radio. Filter by filter_type (all/wired/wireless), set include_offline=true for historical clients. For a single client's full raw object, use unifi_get_client_details. For IP-to-hostname lookup, use unifi_lookup_by_ip.
tools.name: unifi_list_clients

filter_type.description: Filter clients by connection type: 'all' (default), 'wired', or 'wireless'

include_offline.description: When true, includes offline/disconnected clients from controller history. Default false

limit.description: Maximum number of clients to return (default 100)

tools.description: List content filtering profiles. Profiles control DNS-based category blocking, safe search enforcement (GOOGLE, YOUTUBE, BING), and domain allow/block lists. Profiles can target specific clients by MAC or entire networks by ID. NOTE: Profiles must be created in the UniFi UI — the API only supports list, update, and delete. Common categories: FAMILY, ADVERTISEMENT, MALWARE, PHISHING, BOTNETS, SPAM, SPYWARE, HACKING, ANONYMIZERS, DNS_TUNNELING, ADULT, ALCOHOL, DRUGS, GAMBLING, VIOLENCE, PORNOGRAPHY, NUDITY, WEAPONS, DATING, HATE_SPEECH_AND_EXTREMISM, CHILD_ABUSE, CIPA, EMPTY_DOMAINS, NEWLY_DISCOVERED_DOMAINS, PARKED_DOMAINS, UNREACHABLE_DOMAINS.
tools.name: unifi_list_content_filters

tools.description: Returns adopted device inventory with MAC, name, model, IP, firmware version, uptime, status (online/offline/upgrading/etc), device_category (ap/switch/gateway/pdu), upgradable flag, connection_network, uplink topology, load_avg, mem_pct, and model_eol. Filter by device_type (ap/switch/gateway/pdu) and status (online/offline/pending/upgrading). Note: device_type=ap correctly excludes USP Smart Power strips. Set include_details=true for radio tables, port tables, and client counts. For a single device's full raw object, use unifi_get_device_details.
tools.name: unifi_list_devices

device_type.description: Filter by device type: 'all' (default), 'ap' (access points), 'switch', 'gateway', or 'pdu'

include_details.description: When true, includes additional details like radio tables (APs), port tables (switches), WAN info (gateways), and client counts. Default false

tools.description: List all static DNS records configured on the controller. Returns hostname, value, record type, enabled state, and TTL for each record.
tools.name: unifi_list_dns_records

tools.description: List DPI applications available for use in firewall rules and OON policies. Returns application names and their compound IDs. Supports name-based search. NOTE: The official API currently only returns categories 0-1 (IM, P2P). Streaming, social media, and other categories are not yet populated by Ubiquiti. For apps not found here, add via the UniFi UI and read back the IDs from the policy. Requires UNIFI_API_KEY or UNIFI_NETWORK_API_KEY.
tools.name: unifi_list_dpi_applications

search.description: Search for apps by name (case-insensitive, client-side filter). E.g., 'slack', 'telegram'

tools.description: List DPI application categories (e.g., 'Instant messengers', 'Peer-to-peer networks', 'Media streaming services'). Categories group applications for DPI classification. Requires UNIFI_API_KEY or UNIFI_NETWORK_API_KEY.
tools.name: unifi_list_dpi_categories

tools.description: Returns timestamped event log entries (client connects/disconnects, device state changes, firmware updates, config changes) sorted newest-first. Filter by within_hours (default 24), event_type prefix (use unifi_get_event_types for valid prefixes), and paginate with start/limit. For critical alerts specifically, use unifi_list_alarms instead.
tools.name: unifi_list_events

event_type.description: Filter by event type prefix (e.g., 'EVT_WU_' for wireless user events, 'EVT_SW_' for switch events). Use unifi_get_event_types to see valid prefixes

limit.description: Maximum number of events to return (default 100)

start.description: Offset for pagination, skip the first N events (default 0)

within_hours.description: Only return events from the last N hours (default 24)

tools.description: List firewall groups (address and port groups) used as reusable objects in firewall policies. Address groups contain IP addresses/CIDRs, port groups contain port numbers/ranges. These are referenced by firewall policies via ip_group_id and port_group_id fields.
tools.name: unifi_list_firewall_groups

tools.description: List firewall policies configured on the Unifi Network controller. Includes zone-based targeting details (zone_id, matching_target, matching_target_type, IPs, network IDs) when present on newer firmware.
tools.name: unifi_list_firewall_policies

include_predefined.description: When true, includes predefined system policies in results. Default false (user-defined only)

tools.description: List controller firewall zones (V2 API).
tools.name: unifi_list_firewall_zones

tools.description: List APs you have previously classified as known/acknowledged. These are neighboring APs that have been reviewed and marked as not a threat. Returns the full list of known rogue AP entries.
tools.name: unifi_list_known_rogue_aps

tools.description: Returns all configured networks (LAN, WAN, VLAN-only) with name, purpose, IP subnet, VLAN ID, DHCP settings, and enabled state. Use to understand network topology and VLAN layout. For a single network's full config, use unifi_get_network_details. For wireless SSIDs, use unifi_list_wlans.
tools.name: unifi_list_networks

tools.description: List OON (Object-Oriented Network) policies. These policies control internet access schedules (bedtime blackouts), application blocking (social media, streaming), QoS, and policy-based routing.
tools.name: unifi_list_oon_policies

tools.description: List all port forwarding rules on your Unifi Network controller.
tools.name: unifi_list_port_forwards

tools.description: List all port profiles (port configurations) on the controller. Port profiles define VLAN assignment, port isolation, PoE mode, STP, 802.1X, and storm control settings. Profiles are assigned to switch ports via port overrides.
tools.name: unifi_list_port_profiles

tools.description: List all QoS rules on the Unifi Network controller for the current site.
tools.name: unifi_list_qos_rules

tools.description: List neighboring/rogue APs detected by your access points. Returns BSSID, SSID, channel, RSSI, band, and which of your APs detected each one. Useful for RF environment analysis and interference troubleshooting. WARNING: This can return hundreds of APs. Use channel and/or min_signal filters to reduce the result set.
tools.name: unifi_list_rogue_aps

channel.description: Filter to a specific channel (e.g., 1, 6, 11 for 2.4GHz; 36, 100 for 5GHz)

min_signal.description: Minimum signal strength in dBm (e.g., -70 to only show strong neighbors)

within_hours.description: Hours to look back for detected APs (default 24)

tools.description:
List all user-defined static routes for the current site.

Returns route names, destination networks, next-hop addresses, and status.
These are manually configured routes, not dynamic or system routes.
tools.name: unifi_list_routes

tools.description:
List all traffic routes (policy-based routing rules) for the current site.

Traffic routes define how specific traffic is routed based on domains,
IP addresses, regions, or target devices. Often used for VPN routing.
tools.name: unifi_list_traffic_routes

tools.description:
List all user groups (bandwidth profiles) for the current site.

User groups define bandwidth limits that can be applied to clients.
Each group specifies upload/download speed caps in Kbps.
tools.name: unifi_list_usergroups

tools.description:
List all hotspot vouchers for the current site.

Returns voucher codes, expiration times, usage quotas, and bandwidth limits.
Vouchers are used for guest network access in captive portal setups.
tools.name: unifi_list_vouchers

tools.description: List all configured VPN clients (Wireguard, OpenVPN, etc).
tools.name: unifi_list_vpn_clients

tools.description: List all configured VPN servers (Wireguard, OpenVPN, L2TP, etc).
tools.name: unifi_list_vpn_servers

tools.description: List WireGuard peers (VPN clients). Optionally filter by server_id to list peers for a specific WireGuard server. Returns peer name, ID, assigned IP, and public key. Private keys are redacted.
tools.name: unifi_list_wireguard_peers

server_id.description: Optional WireGuard server _id to filter peers (from unifi_list_vpn_servers). If omitted, returns peers across all WireGuard servers.

tools.description: List all configured Wireless LANs (WLANs) on the Unifi Network controller.
tools.name: unifi_list_wlans

tools.description: Toggle device locate mode (LED blinking) to physically identify a device. Works on any UniFi device (switches, APs, gateways). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_locate_device

confirm.description: When true, toggles locate mode. When false (default), returns a preview

device_mac.description: MAC address of the device (from unifi_list_devices)

enabled.description: True to start blinking, False to stop

tools.description: Quick IP-to-hostname lookup. Returns only essential fields (hostname, name, MAC) to minimize token usage.
tools.name: unifi_lookup_by_ip

tools.description: Power cycle PoE on a specific switch port. This briefly cuts power to the connected PoE device, causing it to reboot. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_power_cycle_port

confirm.description: When true, power cycles the port. When false (default), returns a preview

tools.description: Reboot a specific device by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_reboot_device

confirm.description: When true, executes the reboot. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the device to reboot, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices)

tools.description: Rename a client/device in the Unifi Network controller by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_rename_client

confirm.description: When true, executes the rename. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to rename, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

name.description: New display name for the client (e.g., 'Living Room TV')

tools.description: Rename a device in the Unifi Network controller by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_rename_device

confirm.description: When true, executes the rename. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the device to rename, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices)

name.description: New display name for the device (e.g., 'Office AP')

tools.description: Revoke/delete a hotspot voucher by its ID, preventing further use
tools.name: unifi_revoke_voucher

confirm.description: When true, revokes the voucher. When false (default), returns a preview

voucher_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the voucher to revoke (from unifi_list_vouchers)

tools.description:
Set fixed IP address and/or local DNS record for a client device.

Allows configuring:
- Fixed IP: Assign a static IP address to a client (DHCP reservation)
- Local DNS: Create a local DNS hostname for the client (UniFi Network 7.2+)

Either setting can be enabled/disabled independently.
tools.name: unifi_set_client_ip_settings

confirm.description: When true, applies the IP settings. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

fixed_ip.description: Static IP address to assign (e.g., '192.168.1.50'). Only used when use_fixedip is true

local_dns_record.description: Local DNS hostname for this client (e.g., 'mydevice.local'). Only used when local_dns_record_enabled is true

local_dns_record_enabled.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) local DNS record for this client (UniFi Network 7.2+)

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to configure, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_clients)

use_fixedip.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) fixed IP / DHCP reservation for this client

tools.description: Set the LED override state on a specific device. Use 'on' to force LEDs on, 'off' to force them off, or 'default' to use site-wide setting. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_set_device_led

confirm.description: When true, applies the LED change. When false (default), returns a preview

device_mac.description: MAC address of the device (from unifi_list_devices)

led_state.description: LED override state: 'on' (force on), 'off' (force off), or 'default' (use site setting)

tools.description: Enable or disable jumbo frames on a switch. Note: the UniFi controller requires jumbo frames to be enabled via the UI at least once before the API can toggle it. If you get JumboFrameChangeNotAllowed, enable it in the UI first. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_set_jumbo_frames

confirm.description: When true, applies the change. When false (default), returns a preview

enabled.description: True to enable jumbo frames, False to disable

tools.description: Toggle all device LEDs site-wide on or off. This sets the global LED policy for the site — individual device overrides take precedence. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_set_site_leds

confirm.description: When true, applies the change. When false (default), returns a preview

enabled.description: True to enable all site LEDs, False to disable them

tools.description: Assign a port profile to a specific switch port. IMPORTANT: This sends ALL port overrides as a full replacement. Fetch current overrides with unifi_get_switch_ports first, modify the target port, and send the complete array. Missing ports revert to defaults. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_set_switch_port_profile

confirm.description: When true, applies the changes. When false (default), returns a preview

port_overrides.description: Complete list of port overrides. Each entry needs at minimum: port_idx (int, 1-based), portconf_id (str, profile ID). Optional: name, poe_mode, op_mode ('switch'/'mirror'/'aggregate')

tools.description: Enable or disable a device without unadopting it. A disabled device remains adopted but stops passing traffic. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_device

confirm.description: When true, applies the change. When false (default), returns a preview

device_mac.description: MAC address of the device (from unifi_list_devices)

disabled.description: True to disable the device, False to enable it

tools.description: Enable or disable a specific firewall policy by ID.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_firewall_policy

confirm.description: When true, executes the toggle. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

policy_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the firewall policy to toggle (from unifi_list_firewall_policies)

tools.description: Toggle an OON policy on or off. Fetches the current state, flips the enabled flag, and sends the update. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_oon_policy

confirm.description: When true, toggles the policy. When false (default), returns a preview

policy_id.description: The ID of the policy to toggle

tools.description: Toggle a port forwarding rule on or off on your Unifi Network controller.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_port_forward

confirm.description: When true, executes the toggle. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

port_forward_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the port forwarding rule to toggle (from unifi_list_port_forwards)

tools.description: Enable or disable a specific QoS rule by ID. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_qos_rule_enabled

confirm.description: When true, executes the toggle. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

rule_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the QoS rule to toggle (from unifi_list_qos_rules)

tools.description: Toggle a traffic route on/off by ID.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_traffic_route

confirm.description: When true, executes the toggle. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

route_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the traffic route to toggle (from unifi_list_traffic_routes)

tools.description: Toggle a WLAN/SSID on or off. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_toggle_wlan

confirm.description: When true, executes the toggle. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

wlan_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WLAN/SSID to toggle (from unifi_list_wlans)

tools.description: Trigger an RF spectrum scan on an access point. Requires confirmation. The scan takes 5-10 minutes to complete. Some APs have a dedicated scanning radio and scan without going offline; others briefly go off-channel which may cause momentary client disconnections. Poll unifi_get_rf_scan_results periodically — results appear only after the scan finishes. Returns per-channel interference levels and utilization across all bands.
tools.name: unifi_trigger_rf_scan

ap_mac.description: MAC address of the access point to scan, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices with device_type='ap')

confirm.description: When true, triggers the RF scan. When false (default), returns a preview. WARNING: AP briefly goes off-channel during scan

tools.description: Trigger a speedtest on the gateway device. Returns immediately; use unifi_get_speedtest_status to poll for results.
tools.name: unifi_trigger_speedtest

confirm.description: When true, triggers the speedtest. When false (default), returns a preview.

gateway_mac.description: MAC address of the gateway device

tools.description: Revoke authorization for a guest client by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_unauthorize_guest

confirm.description: When true, revokes guest authorization. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the guest client to unauthorize, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF

tools.description: Unblock a previously blocked client/device by MAC address
tools.name: unifi_unblock_client

confirm.description: When true, executes the unblock. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the client to unblock, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_blocked_clients)

tools.description: Update an existing MAC ACL rule. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Uses the same field names as unifi_list_acl_rules output: name, acl_index, action, enabled, network_id, source_macs, destination_macs. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_acl_rule

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

rule_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Allowed keys: name, acl_index, action ('ALLOW'/'BLOCK'), enabled (bool), network_id, source_macs (list of MACs), destination_macs (list of MACs)

rule_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the ACL rule to update (from unifi_list_acl_rules)

tools.description: Update an existing AP group's configuration. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_ap_group

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

group_id.description: Unique identifier of the AP group to update (from unifi_list_ap_groups)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Common fields: name (str), device_macs (list), wlan_group_ids (list)

tools.description: Update auto-backup settings. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Fields: autobackup_enabled (bool), autobackup_cron_expr (str, cron format), autobackup_days (int, retention days), autobackup_max_files (int, max backup files to keep), autobackup_timezone (str), autobackup_cloud_enabled (bool). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_autobackup_settings

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview

update_data.description: Dictionary of auto-backup fields to update. See tool description for supported fields.

tools.description: Update an existing client group. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_client_group

confirm.description: When true, updates the group. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

group_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Allowed keys: name (str), members (list of MAC addresses)

group_id.description: The ID of the group to update

tools.description: Update an existing content filtering profile. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. client_macs and network_ids are additive — both can be set and the filter applies to all. Safe search valid values: GOOGLE, YOUTUBE, BING (only these three are supported). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_content_filter

confirm.description: When true, updates the profile. When false (default), returns a preview

filter_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Allowed keys: name, enabled (bool), blocked_categories (list), safe_search (list: 'GOOGLE'/'YOUTUBE'/'BING'), client_macs (list of MACs), network_ids (list)

filter_id.description: The ID of the profile to update

tools.description: Update radio settings for a specific band on an access point. Supports tx_power_mode, tx_power (custom dBm), channel, ht (channel width), min_rssi_enabled, min_rssi, assisted_roaming_enabled (802.11k/v), antenna_gain (dBi), vwire_enabled, sens_level_enabled, sens_level. Use unifi_get_device_radio first to see current settings.
tools.name: unifi_update_device_radio

antenna_gain.description: External antenna gain in dBi for regulatory TX power compensation

assisted_roaming_enabled.description: Enable 802.11k/v assisted roaming (neighbor reports and BSS transition management)

confirm.description: When true, applies radio changes. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the access point, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices)

min_rssi.description: Minimum RSSI value in dBm (e.g., -75). Only valid when min_rssi_enabled=true

min_rssi_enabled.description: Enable minimum RSSI threshold to disconnect weak clients

radio.description: Radio band code to configure: 'na' (5GHz), 'ng' (2.4GHz), '6e'/'wifi6e' (6GHz), or internal name like 'wifi0', 'wifi1'

sens_level.description: Receive sensitivity level in dBm. Only valid when sens_level_enabled=true

tx_power.description: Custom transmit power in dBm (only valid when tx_power_mode='custom')

vwire_enabled.description: Enable virtual wire mode (transparent bridge for meshed APs)

tools.description: Update an existing DNS record. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Fields: key (str), value (str), record_type ('A'/'AAAA'/'CNAME'/'MX'/'TXT'/'SRV'), enabled (bool), ttl (int), port (int), priority (int), weight (int). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_dns_record

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview

record_id.description: The ID of the DNS record to update (from unifi_list_dns_records)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. See tool description for supported fields.

tools.description: Update an existing firewall group. Requires the full group object (PUT replaces entire resource). group_type cannot be changed. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_firewall_group

confirm.description: When true, updates the group. When false (default), returns a preview

group_data.description: The complete updated group object with all fields

group_id.description: The ID of the group to update

tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing firewall policy by ID. Supports both legacy fields (ruleset, action as accept/drop/reject) and zone-based fields (source, destination, action as ALLOW/BLOCK/REJECT, ip_version, etc.).
tools.name: unifi_update_firewall_policy

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

policy_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the firewall policy to update (from unifi_list_firewall_policies)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Accepts both legacy and zone-based fields. Legacy: name, ruleset, action (accept/drop/reject), rule_index, protocol, src_address, dst_address, enabled, description, logging. Zone-based: name, action (ALLOW/BLOCK/REJECT), enabled, source, destination, protocol, ip_version, index, logging, connection_state_type, connection_states, schedule.

tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing network (LAN/VLAN). Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Basic: name, purpose ('corporate'/'guest'/'vlan-only'), vlan_enabled (bool), vlan (str), ip_subnet (CIDR), enabled (bool), network_isolation_enabled (bool, corporate only), internet_access_enabled (bool), upnp_lan_enabled (bool). DHCP: dhcpd_enabled (bool), dhcpd_start (IP), dhcpd_stop (IP), dhcpd_leasetime (int seconds), dhcpd_gateway (IP), dhcpd_gateway_enabled (bool), dhcp_relay_enabled (bool), dhcpd_conflict_checking (bool), dhcpguard_enabled (bool, requires dhcpd_ip_1), dhcpd_ip_1 (IP, trusted DHCP server for guard), dhcpd_boot_enabled (bool), dhcpd_boot_server (IP), dhcpd_boot_filename (str), dhcpd_tftp_server (str, DHCP opt 150). DHCP options: dhcpd_dns_1 (IP), dhcpd_dns_2 (IP), dhcpd_dns_enabled (bool), dhcpd_ntp_1 (IPv4), dhcpd_ntp_2 (IPv4), dhcpd_ntp_enabled (bool), dhcpd_wins_1 (IP), dhcpd_wins_2 (IP), dhcpd_wins_enabled (bool), dhcpd_unifi_controller (IP). DNS: domain_name (str). Multicast: igmp_snooping (bool), igmp_querier_switches (list of {switch_mac, querier_address}), igmp_flood_unknown_multicast (bool), mdns_enabled (bool). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_network

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

network_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the network to update (from unifi_list_networks)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. See tool description for all supported fields.

tools.description: Update an existing OON policy. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_oon_policy

confirm.description: When true, updates the policy. When false (default), returns a preview

policy_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Allowed keys: name (str), enabled (bool), target_type ('CLIENTS'/'GROUPS'), targets (list of MACs or group IDs), secure (dict), qos (dict), route (dict)

policy_id.description: The ID of the policy to update

tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing port forwarding rule using schema validation. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_port_forward

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

port_forward_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the port forwarding rule to update (from unifi_list_port_forwards)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Allowed keys: name, dst_port (external port), fwd_port (internal port), fwd_ip (internal IP), protocol ('tcp'/'udp'/'tcp_udp'), enabled (bool), src_ip (source IP/CIDR, empty string to remove), log (bool)

tools.description: Update an existing port profile. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Note: system profiles with attr_no_edit=true cannot be modified. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_port_profile

confirm.description: When true, updates the profile. When false (default), returns a preview

profile_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Allowed keys: name, forward ('all'/'native'/'customize'/'disabled'), native_networkconf_id, voice_networkconf_id, isolation (bool), poe_mode ('auto'/'off'/'pasv24'/'passthrough'), stp_port_mode (bool), dot1x_ctrl ('force_authorized'/'auto'/'force_unauthorized'/'mac_based'/'multi_host')

profile_id.description: The ID of the profile to update

tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing QoS rule. Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_qos_rule

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

rule_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the QoS rule to update (from unifi_list_qos_rules)

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. Allowed keys: name, interface ('WAN'/'LAN'), direction ('upload'/'download'), bandwidth_limit_kbps (int), target_ip_address (IP), target_subnet (CIDR), dscp_value (0-63), enabled (bool)

tools.description:
Update an existing static route's properties.

Can modify name, destination network, next-hop, distance, or enabled status.
tools.name: unifi_update_route

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

name.description: New descriptive name for the route

network.description: New destination network in CIDR notation (e.g., '10.0.0.0/24')

route_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the static route to update (from unifi_list_routes)

tools.description: Update SNMP settings for the site (enable/disable, set community string). Requires confirm=true to apply changes.
tools.name: unifi_update_snmp_settings

community.description: SNMP community string (e.g., 'public'). Omit to keep the current value

confirm.description: When true, applies the changes. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

enabled.description: Set to true to enable SNMP on the site, false to disable it

tools.description: Update STP (Spanning Tree Protocol) configuration for a switch. stp_priority valid values: 4096, 8192, 12288, ..., 61440 (default: 32768). stp_version: 'stp' or 'rstp' (default). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_switch_stp

confirm.description: When true, applies the STP config. When false (default), returns a preview

stp_priority.description: STP bridge priority (4096-61440 in steps of 4096, default 32768)

tools.description:
Update a traffic route's settings.

Can update:
- enabled: Enable or disable the traffic route
- kill_switch_enabled: Enable/disable the kill switch (blocks traffic if VPN is down)

At least one parameter must be provided.
tools.name: unifi_update_traffic_route

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

enabled.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) the traffic route

kill_switch_enabled.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) the kill switch, which blocks traffic if the VPN goes down

route_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the traffic route to update (from unifi_list_traffic_routes)

tools.description:
Update an existing user group's name or bandwidth limits.

Use -1 for unlimited bandwidth. Changes affect all clients assigned to this group.
tools.name: unifi_update_usergroup

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

down_limit_kbps.description: New download speed limit in Kbps. Use -1 for unlimited

group_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the user group to update (from unifi_list_usergroups)

name.description: New name for the user group

up_limit_kbps.description: New upload speed limit in Kbps. Use -1 for unlimited

tools.description: Enable or disable a specific VPN client by ID.
tools.name: unifi_update_vpn_client_state

client_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the VPN client to update (from unifi_list_vpn_clients)

enabled.description: Set to true to enable the VPN client, false to disable it

tools.description: Enable or disable a specific VPN server by ID.
tools.name: unifi_update_vpn_server_state

enabled.description: Set to true to enable the VPN server, false to disable it

server_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the VPN server to update (from unifi_list_vpn_servers)

tools.description: Update specific fields of an existing WLAN (SSID). Pass only the fields you want to change — current values are automatically preserved. Basic: name (str), security ('open'/'wpapsk'/'wpa2-psk'), x_passphrase (str), enabled (bool), hide_ssid (bool), guest_policy (bool), usergroup_id (str), networkconf_id (str). Security: wpa3_support (bool), wpa3_transition (bool), pmf_mode ('disabled'/'optional'/'required'), fast_roaming_enabled (bool), group_rekey (int seconds, 0=disabled). Access control: mac_filter_enabled (bool), mac_filter_policy ('allow'/'deny'), mac_filter_list (list of MAC strings), l2_isolation (bool). Radio: wlan_band ('both'/'2g'/'5g'), multicast_enhance_enabled (bool), dtim_mode ('default'/'custom'), dtim_na (int 1-255), dtim_ng (int 1-255), minrate_ng_enabled (bool), minrate_ng_data_rate_kbps (int), minrate_na_enabled (bool), minrate_na_data_rate_kbps (int). Other: schedule_enabled (bool), uapsd_enabled (bool), proxy_arp (bool), iapp_enabled (bool). Requires confirmation.
tools.name: unifi_update_wlan

confirm.description: When true, applies the update. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

update_data.description: Dictionary of fields to update. See tool description for all supported fields.

wlan_id.description: Unique identifier (_id) of the WLAN to update (from unifi_list_wlans)

tools.description: Initiate a firmware upgrade for a device by MAC address (uses cached firmware by default)
tools.name: unifi_upgrade_device

confirm.description: When true, initiates the firmware upgrade. When false (default), returns a preview of the changes

mac_address.description: MAC address of the device to upgrade, in format AA:BB:CC:DD:EE:FF (from unifi_list_devices)
